name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY)
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: A flagship credit-linked subsidy and direct financial assistance scheme to help low, middle-income families, and economically weaker sections build or purchase their first permanent (Pucca) house. Covers urban and rural developments.
benefits: Interest subsidy up to 6.5% on home loans (up to Rs. 6 Lakh loan amount)
Direct central assistance of Rs. 1.5 Lakh for house construction in urban areas
Special preference given to women, senior citizens, and physically challenged applicants
Mandatory joint ownership with female head of the family to promote women empowerment
eligibilityCriteria: Beneficiary family must not own a permanent (Pucca) house anywhere in India in the name of any family member.
The beneficiary family must comprise husband, wife, and unmarried children.
Annual household income must fall within: EWS (< Rs. 3 Lakh), LIG (Rs. 3-6 Lakh), MIG-I (Rs. 6-12 Lakh), or MIG-II (Rs. 12-18 Lakh).
amountDetails: Interest subsidy range: 3.00% to 6.50% based on income tier. Max subsidy value: Rs. 2.67 Lakh. Direct construction grant: Rs. 1.50 Lakh.
applicationSteps: Go to the official PMAY portal (pmaymis.gov.in) and register using Aadhaar card details.
Fill out the application form with personal, family, income, and bank details.
Upload scans of Aadhaar, Income Certificate, and property/land documents.
Submit and download the application receipt containing your unique Application ID.
extraIntel: Toll-free helpline numbers: 1800-11-3377, 1800-11-6111.
Ensure the property title has the female head of the household listed as the owner or co-owner.
disbursementSchedule: Loan subsidy credited at disbursement; direct construction grants in 3 installments based on physical progress.

name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: A direct income support scheme providing assured financial assistance to all landholding farmer families across India to help buy agricultural inputs and meet domestic needs.
benefits: Assured direct income support of Rs. 6,000 per year
Transferred in three equal installments of Rs. 2,000 every 4 months
Direct benefit transfer (DBT) directly into Aadhaar-seeded bank accounts
Covers all landholding farmer families regardless of land size
eligibilityCriteria: All small and marginal landholder farmer families who own cultivable land.
Must have land registered in their name (mutation of land records completed).
Excludes institutional landholders, government employees, income taxpayers, and professionals (doctors, lawyers, etc.).
amountDetails: Rs. 6,000 per year, paid in 3 installments of Rs. 2,000 each (typically April-July, August-November, and December-March).
applicationSteps: Visit PM Kisan portal (pmkisan.gov.in) and click on 'New Farmer Registration'.
Enter Aadhaar and mobile number, verify via OTP, and select your state/district.
Fill in land survey/Khasra numbers and bank account details.
Upload land registry documents and submit for local patwari verification.
extraIntel: PM-Kisan Helpline: 155261 or 1800115526.
Your bank account must be Aadhaar-seeded and e-KYC must be completed on the portal to receive transfers.
disbursementSchedule: Thrice a year: Installment 1 (April-July), Installment 2 (August-November), Installment 3 (December-March).
benefitDuration: Ongoing (As long as landholding criteria are met)

name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (MUDRA)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: Provides collateral-free institutional loans up to Rs. 10 Lakh to help micro, small business owners, and startup founders start, expand, or modernize non-corporate, non-farm businesses.
benefits: No collateral or third-party guarantee required for any loan tier
Three customized categories: Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishor (up to Rs. 5 Lakh), and Tarun (up to Rs. 10 Lakh)
Low processing fees and flexible repayment periods up to 5 years
Mudra Card provided for easy working capital cash withdrawals
eligibilityCriteria: Any Indian citizen who has a business plan for a non-farm sector income-generating activity.
Business must be in manufacturing, trading, or service sectors.
Proprietorships, partnerships, and small private companies are eligible.
amountDetails: Loan amount categories: Shishu (up to Rs. 50,000), Kishor (Rs. 50,001 to Rs. 5,00,000), Tarun (Rs. 5,00,001 to Rs. 10,00,000). Interest rates vary between 8.60% to 12.00% depending on the lending bank.
applicationSteps: Prepare a business plan and select the Mudra loan category (Shishu, Kishor, or Tarun).
Approach any public/private commercial bank, regional rural bank, or cooperative bank.
Submit the Mudra Loan Application form along with identity, address, and business proofs.
Upon approval, bank issues a Mudra Debit Card to manage working capital.
extraIntel: Mudra National Helpline: 1800-180-1111 / 1800-11-0001.
Avoid brokers. Mudra loans are collateral-free and do not require processing fees for the Shishu category.
disbursementSchedule: Lump sum credit to Mudra account or business cash credit line on approval.
nextMilestoneDate: Instant processing (typically 7-15 days for Shishu category)

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: A small deposit savings scheme for girl children, launched as part of the 'Beti Bachao Beti Padhao' campaign. Offers high tax-free interest to build a fund for education and marriage.
benefits: Highest government-backed interest rate among small savings schemes
Triple tax exemptions: No tax on investment, interest earned, or final maturity amount (Section 80C)
Minimum deposit of just Rs. 250 per year, making it affordable for everyone
Partial withdrawal allowed for higher education expenses after the girl turns 18
eligibilityCriteria: Account can only be opened by a natural parent or legal guardian in the name of a girl child.
The girl child must be an Indian resident and aged 10 years or younger at the time of opening.
Only one account is allowed per girl child, with a maximum of two accounts per family.
amountDetails: Deposits range from minimum Rs. 250 to maximum Rs. 1.5 Lakh per fiscal year. Interest rate is set quarterly (typically 8.2% in 2026), compounded annually.
applicationSteps: Go to your nearest Post Office or authorized commercial bank branch.
Fill out the SSY account opening form (Form-I).
Submit the girl child's birth certificate, guardian's Aadhaar, PAN card, and photos.
Deposit the initial opening amount (minimum Rs. 250) to activate the account passbook.
extraIntel: Toll-free Post Office Helpline: 1800-266-6868.
Deposits must be made for 15 years from account opening. The account matures 21 years from the date of opening.
disbursementSchedule: Maturity disbursement 21 years from account opening, or partial withdrawal at age 18 for higher education.
nextMilestoneDate: Annual interest credited: March 31st (end of fiscal year)
benefitDuration: 21 Years (or marriage of the girl child after age 18)
lastDateToApply: Before the girl child reaches 10 years of age

name: Ayushman Bharat (PM-JAY)
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
description: The world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme. Provides completely free secondary and tertiary care hospitalization cover to poor and vulnerable families.
benefits: Cashless and paperless health insurance cover of Rs. 5 Lakh per family per year
Covers all pre-existing medical conditions from day one of enrollment
No cap on family size, age of members, or gender of the beneficiaries
Covers up to 3 days of pre-hospitalization and 15 days of post-hospitalization costs (including medicines)
eligibilityCriteria: Families listed under the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) database.
Families holding valid BPL or Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) ration cards.
Includes occupational categories such as ragpickers, beggars, domestic workers, street vendors, and manual laborers.
amountDetails: Rs. 5,00,000 per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization on a family floater basis (no limit on individual member usage).
applicationSteps: Go to the PM-JAY website (pmjay.gov.in) and click 'Am I Eligible' to check status.
If listed, visit the nearest Ayushman Mitra desk at any empanelled hospital.
Present your Aadhaar Card and Ration Card to complete biometric e-KYC.
Receive your golden Ayushman Card to get cashless treatment immediately.
extraIntel: National Health Helpline: 14555 or 1800-111-565.
The scheme covers both government and empanelled private hospitals. Check the hospital directory on the portal.
disbursementSchedule: Cashless claim settled directly with hospital at discharge.

name: Skill India Mission (PMKVY)
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: A skill certification scheme aiming to enable Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training that helps secure a better livelihood and job placement.
benefits: 100% free short-term skill training in multiple technical and services sectors
Government-recognized Skill Certificate and Assessment Card on completion
Placement assistance, job fairs, and entrepreneurship guidance support
Pravasi Kaushal Vikas Yojana option for overseas job seekers
eligibilityCriteria: Unemployed youth, school dropouts, or college graduates seeking employability skills.
Must be an Indian national aged between 15 and 45 years.
Must possess a basic bank account and active Aadhaar card.
amountDetails: 100% free course fees, examination fees, and study material. Offers monetary rewards (up to Rs. 8,000) or reward points on successful training completion and placement.
applicationSteps: Visit the Skill India portal (skillindia.gov.in) and register as a candidate.
Find and select a recognized PMKVY training partner center near your location.
Enroll in your desired course sector (e.g. IT, Electronics, Healthcare, Retail).
Complete the training, pass the final assessment, and collect your Skill India Certificate.
extraIntel: DigiLocker hosts digital copies of Skill India Certificates for instant resume verification.
disbursementSchedule: Training fees paid directly to centers; candidate reward incentives credited within 30 days of certificate issue.

name: Startup India Initiative
ministry: DPIIT
description: An action plan designed to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation, driving sustainable economic growth, and generating large-scale employment opportunities for startups.
benefits: Income tax exemption for 3 consecutive block years out of the first 10 years
Self-certification compliance for labor laws and environmental inspections
80% rebate on patent filing fees and 50% rebate on trademark applications
Easy winding up process (within 90 days) under the Insolvency Code
eligibilityCriteria: The company must be incorporated as a Private Limited, LL Partnership, or Registered Partnership.
Must not exceed an annual turnover of Rs. 100 Crore in any financial year.
Must be registered in India for less than 10 years from the date of incorporation.
amountDetails: Tax benefits: 100% tax exemption under Section 80-IAC. Patent rebate: 80% filing cost reduction. Startup seed fund grants: up to Rs. 20 Lakh for proof of concept and Rs. 50 Lakh for commercialization.
applicationSteps: Incorporate your business entities on the MCA portal (mca.gov.in).
Create an account on the Startup India portal (startupindia.gov.in).
Submit the DPIIT recognition application form with incorporation certificate and business write-up.
Download your official DPIIT Recognition Certificate once verified.
extraIntel: Startup India Toll-free helpline: 1800-115-565.
Ensure your startup is working towards innovation, development, or improvement of products, processes, or services.
disbursementSchedule: Rebates credited at application; seed funding disbursed in stages milestones by incubator boards.
nextMilestoneDate: DPIIT certificate processing: 2-4 weeks from submission

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: A pension scheme targeted at unorganized sector workers (like maids, drivers, delivery partners). Offers a guaranteed monthly pension to secure retirement years.
benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension of Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,000, Rs. 3,000, or Rs. 5,000 after age 60
Spouse receives the exact same pension amount automatically upon subscriber's death
Maturity corpus handed over to the nominee after both subscriber and spouse pass away
Auto-debit facility from savings bank account with flexible contribution upgrades
eligibilityCriteria: Must be an Indian citizen aged between 18 and 40 years.
Must hold a savings bank account with auto-debit facility enabled.
Must not be an active taxpayer or subscriber of any other statutory social security scheme (e.g. EPF, NPS).
amountDetails: Monthly pension tiers: Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,000, Rs. 3,000, or Rs. 5,000. Contributions depend on your age of entry (e.g., starting at age 18 requires Rs. 42/month for a Rs. 1,000 pension).
applicationSteps: Approach the bank/post office branch where you hold your savings bank account.
Fill out the APY registration form with nominee details and choose your pension amount.
Submit copies of your Aadhaar card and passbook.
Authorize auto-debit of monthly/quarterly contributions from your bank account.
extraIntel: NPS/APY Helpline: 1800-110-708.
Start early. The monthly contribution amount increases significantly if you enroll closer to age 40.
disbursementSchedule: Monthly pension paid out starting from the 1st of the month following the subscriber's 60th birthday.

name: PM SVANidhi
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: A micro-credit loan scheme providing affordable working capital loans to street vendors to help restart their livelihoods, promote digital transactions, and integrate vendors into the formal economy.
benefits: Initial collateral-free working capital loan up to Rs. 10,000, progressing to Rs. 20,000 and Rs. 50,000 upon timely repayment
Interest subsidy of 7% per annum credited directly into bank accounts on regular repayments
Cashback incentives up to Rs. 1,200 per year on conducting digital transactions
No prepayment penalty for clearing the loan before the scheduled tenure
eligibilityCriteria: Street vendors vending in urban areas on or before March 24, 2020.
Must possess a Certificate of Vending (CoV) or Identity Card issued by the local Urban Local Body (ULB).
Vendors left out of ULB surveys must possess a Letter of Recommendation (LoR) issued by the TVC/Municipality.
amountDetails: First loan: up to Rs. 10,000 (12-month tenure). Second loan: up to Rs. 20,000 (24-month tenure). Third loan: up to Rs. 50,000 (36-month tenure). 7% interest subsidy is credited quarterly.
applicationSteps: Visit the PM SVANidhi portal (pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in) or approach your nearest CSC.
Click 'Apply for Loan' and enter your mobile number linked with Aadhaar.
Input your Vending ID/LoR number and fill out the vendor profile details.
Select your lending institution, submit, and await bank field agent inspection.
extraIntel: PM SVANidhi Helpline: 1800-111-979.
Conducting transactions digitally using UPI (PhonePe, Paytm, etc.) earns you cashback rewards that can cover your monthly interest.
disbursementSchedule: Full loan value disbursed into the vendor's bank account within 3-5 days of bank clearance.
nextMilestoneDate: Interest subsidy payout: End of each financial quarter
benefitDuration: 1 to 3 Years depending on loan tier

name: Post Matric Scholarship Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: Provides financial support to SC, ST, and OBC students to complete post-matriculation or post-secondary courses. Minimizes dropouts at the higher education stage.
benefits: 100% tuition fees and compulsory non-refundable fees reimbursed by the government
Monthly maintenance allowance up to Rs. 1,200 for hostellers and Rs. 550 for day scholars
Additional allowances provided for study tours, thesis typing, and disabled students
Online direct bank transfer of scholarship amounts into students' bank accounts
eligibilityCriteria: Must belong to Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), or Other Backward Class (OBC) categories.
Must be pursuing post-matriculation (Class 11, Class 12, Diploma, Degree, or PG) courses in a recognized institution.
Annual family income from all sources must not exceed Rs. 2,500,000.
amountDetails: Full reimbursement of tuition fees. Monthly maintenance allowance: Group I courses (Professional/Medical/Engg) Rs. 1,200/month; Group II (PG/UG Diploma) Rs. 820/month; Group III (General Graduate) Rs. 570/month; Group IV (Class 11/12) Rs. 380/month.
applicationSteps: Register as a student on the National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in).
Fill out the application form specifying your university, course, and category details.
Upload scanned copies of caste, income, bank passbook, and paid tuition fee receipt.
Submit the application online and verify with your college administration office for portal verification.
extraIntel: NSP Helpdesk Helpline: 0120-6619540.
Applications must be verified at both institute and state levels on the portal. Track your status under the 'Track Application' tab.
disbursementSchedule: One-time annual direct bank transfer (DBT) post college and state verification approvals.

name: PM Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
description: Provides clean cooking fuel (LPG connections) to women from economically backward households. Replaces traditional firewood and coal fuels, protecting health and the environment.
benefits: Completely free first LPG gas cylinder and double-burner stove connection
Financial assistance of Rs. 1,600 provided for connection administrative charges
Interest-free loans available to purchase hot plates and pay for subsequent refills
Distributions done directly to the female head of BPL/SECC households
eligibilityCriteria: Applicant must be a female resident of India aged 18 years or older.
Must belong to an economically weaker household (listed in SECC, SC/ST, or hold BPL Ration Card).
No other family member in the same household must own an active LPG gas connection.
amountDetails: Direct grant of Rs. 1,600 to cover gas regulator, pipe, and registration. Free 14.2 kg LPG cylinder (first refill) and double-burner cooking stove.
applicationSteps: Approach your nearest LPG gas distributor (Indane, HP Gas, or Bharat Gas).
Fill out the PM Ujjwala Yojana KYC Application form.
Submit documents including Aadhaar, Ration Card, Bank Passbook, and passport photos.
Once approved, the distributor installs the cylinder and stove at your residence.
extraIntel: PMUY Ujjwala Helpline: 1800-266-6696 or 1906.
Subsequent cylinder refills can be purchased on subsidized interest-free EMI loans deducted from government subsidy transfers.
disbursementSchedule: Instant physical connection allocation at distributor depot upon registration verification.
benefitDuration: One-time initial connection allocation (Subsequent refills subsidized)

name: PM Vishwakarma Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
description: Support for traditional artisans and craftspeople using hands and tools in 18 trades, providing recognition, skill upgrades, toolkit incentives, and collateral-free credit.
benefits: Recognition through PM Vishwakarma Certificate and ID Card
Skill upgradation training (5-7 days basic, 15+ days advanced) with Rs. 500/day stipend
Toolkit incentive of Rs. 15,000 grant
Collateral-free enterprise development loans up to Rs. 3 Lakh at 5% interest rate
eligibilityCriteria: An artisan or craftsperson working with hands and tools in one of the 18 family-based traditional trades.
Minimum age of 18 years on the date of registration.
Only one member of the family can receive benefits. Government servants are excluded.
amountDetails: Rs. 15,000 toolkit grant. Collateral-free credit support of Rs. 1 Lakh (1st tranche) and Rs. 2 Lakh (2nd tranche) at an attractive interest rate of 5%.
applicationSteps: Visit PM Vishwakarma portal (pmvishwakarma.gov.in) and register using Aadhaar authentication.
Complete the mobile and Aadhaar biometric e-KYC.
Apply for the Vishwakarma digital ID Card and certificate.
Submit enterprise loan application or toolkit incentive request.
extraIntel: Includes 18 trades such as Carpenter, Boat Maker, Blacksmith, Potter, Sculptor, Cobbler, Tailor, and Weaver.
disbursementSchedule: Direct benefit transfer for stipends/grants; loan tranches disbursed upon training completion.

name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: A crop insurance scheme to provide financial support to farmers suffering crop loss or damage due to natural calamities, pests, or diseases, stabilizing their income.
benefits: Comprehensive insurance cover against crop failure from sowing to post-harvest
Very low premium rates for farmers: 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, and 5% for horticultural crops
Direct claim settlements credited directly into bank accounts
Coverage for localized calamities like hailstorm, landslide, and inundation
eligibilityCriteria: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing notified crops in notified areas.
Must have insurable interest in the cultivated crop.
amountDetails: Claim amount based on threshold yield and actual crop damage assessment, up to 100% of sum insured value.
applicationSteps: Log in to Crop Insurance portal (pmfby.gov.in) or visit nearest bank/CSC.
Enter farmer details, land survey numbers, and crop sown details.
Pay the nominal farmer premium share online or authorize bank auto-deduction.
Submit and download the policy receipt detailing sum insured.
extraIntel: PMFBY Helpline: 1800-180-1551.
Loss must be reported within 72 hours of occurrence to the insurance company or local agriculture office to claim localized damage benefits.
disbursementSchedule: Claim settlements processed within 30 days of yield data submission by state governments.
lastDateToApply: July 31st for Kharif / December 31st for Rabi annually

name: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
ministry: Ministry of Labour and Employment
description: A voluntary and co-contributory pension scheme for unorganized workers (like street vendors, rickshaw pullers, construction laborers) to ensure financial security in old age.
benefits: Guaranteed minimum monthly pension of Rs. 3,000 after attaining age 60
50% spouse pension benefits upon death of subscriber
Equal co-contribution by the Central Government directly into the pension fund
Simple exit options with interest returns if subscriber exits early
eligibilityCriteria: Must be an unorganized worker aged between 18 and 40 years.
Monthly income must be Rs. 15,000 or less.
Must not be covered under organized social security (EPF, ESIC, NPS) or be an income taxpayer.
amountDetails: Guaranteed pension of Rs. 3,000/month. Monthly contributions range from Rs. 55 to Rs. 200 based on entry age, matched equally by the government.
applicationSteps: Visit the nearest Common Services Center (CSC) or maandhan.in portal.
Enter Aadhaar number and savings bank account number.
Calculate monthly contribution amount and complete biometric authentication.
Receive Shram Yogi Card with unique Pension Account Number.
extraIntel: Labour Toll-free helpline: 1800-267-6888.
Auto-debit is mandatory. Keep sufficient balance in your linked savings bank account every month to avoid interest penalties.
disbursementSchedule: Monthly pension credited starting on the first day of the month following age 60.

name: Lakhpati Didi Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
description: A skill development and financial inclusion program targeting women in Self Help Groups (SHGs) to help them start micro-enterprises and earn a sustainable annual income of at least Rs. 1 Lakh.
benefits: Access to skill development training (LED bulb making, plumbing, drone piloting, sewing, etc.)
Financial assistance and low-interest loan channels through Self Help Groups
Market linkages, branding support, and digital transaction guidance
Drone pilot training (NaMo Drone Didi initiative) for agricultural spraying
eligibilityCriteria: Must be a resident woman member of an active Self Help Group (SHG) registered under NRLM.
Willing to take up skill training and start a micro-enterprise/livelihood activity.
amountDetails: Skill training fully sponsored by the government. Access to community investment funds and bank loans up to Rs. 5 Lakh through SHG networks.
applicationSteps: Contact your local Self Help Group (SHG) head or village organization (VO) coordinator.
Submit application expressing interest in starting a livelihood trade/micro-enterprise.
Attend local block-level skill training workshops sponsored under NRLM.
Apply for loan capital through your SHG group channel to launch the business.
extraIntel: DAY-NRLM Helpline: 1800-110-120.
Includes specialized drone pilot training for women to offer drone rental services to farmers for crop spraying.
disbursementSchedule: Capital grants and loans disbursed to SHG bank accounts on project plan approval.
benefitDuration: Ongoing business support and credit lines

name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: A one-year life insurance scheme renewable from year to year, offering high-value life cover for death due to any reason at a highly affordable annual premium.
benefits: Life insurance cover of Rs. 2 Lakh in case of death of the subscriber due to any reason
Very low premium of just Rs. 436 per year
Direct auto-debit from linked savings bank account
Simple enrollment without any medical examination required
eligibilityCriteria: All savings bank account holders aged between 18 and 50 years.
Must give consent to enable auto-debit of premium from savings bank account.
Individuals who join the scheme before completing 50 years can continue cover up to 55 years.
amountDetails: Rs. 2,00,000 paid to the nominee upon the subscriber's death. Annual premium: Rs. 436.
applicationSteps: Visit the bank branch or post office where you hold your savings account.
Fill out the PMJJBY application and consent-cum-declaration form.
Submit Aadhaar copy and nominee declaration details.
Keep sufficient balance in the account for the annual auto-debit on May 31st.
extraIntel: Jansuraksha Helpline: 1800-180-1111 / 1800-110-001.
A 30-day lien period applies from the date of enrollment, during which claims for natural death are not payable.
disbursementSchedule: Direct claim settlement to the nominee's bank account within 30 days of claim submission.
nextMilestoneDate: Annual auto-debit premium date: May 31st annually

name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: A one-year personal accident insurance scheme offering coverage against accidental death and disability at an extremely low annual premium.
benefits: Accidental death insurance cover of Rs. 2 Lakh
Total and irrecoverable loss of both eyes or loss of use of both hands/feet cover of Rs. 2 Lakh
Partial disability cover of Rs. 1 Lakh for loss of one eye/hand/foot
Extremely affordable premium of just Rs. 20 per year
eligibilityCriteria: All savings bank account holders aged between 18 and 70 years.
Must provide auto-debit consent for premium deduction.
amountDetails: Death / Total disability: Rs. 2,00,000. Partial disability: Rs. 1,00,000. Annual premium: Rs. 20.
applicationSteps: Log into net banking of your account bank or visit nearest branch.
Select 'Social Security Schemes' and enroll in PMSBY.
Provide nominee name and verify Aadhaar registration.
Consent to auto-debit of Rs. 20 annually from savings bank account.
extraIntel: Accidental death includes deaths from drowning, snakebite, slip-and-fall, road accidents, and similar mishaps.
disbursementSchedule: Direct transfer to nominee or claimant's bank account on approval of accident report.

name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: A national mission for financial inclusion, ensuring access to financial services like savings bank accounts, remittances, credit, insurance, and pensions.
benefits: Zero-balance basic savings bank account with no minimum balance requirements
Free RuPay Debit Card with built-in Rs. 2 Lakh accidental insurance cover
Overdraft facility up to Rs. 10,000 for eligible account holders
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) eligibility for all government schemes
eligibilityCriteria: Any Indian citizen who does not hold a bank account.
Minimum age to open account is 10 years.
amountDetails: No minimum balance. Overdraft limit up to Rs. 10,000 based on transaction history. RuPay card accidental cover: Rs. 2,00,000.
applicationSteps: Visit any bank branch or Customer Service Point (CSP) / Bank Mitra.
Fill out the Jan Dhan account opening form.
Provide your Aadhaar card for biometric instant verification.
Receive your account passbook and RuPay debit card instantly.
extraIntel: PMJDY National Toll-free: 1800-11-0001 / 1800-180-1111.
Use your debit card at least once in 90 days to keep the Rs. 2 Lakh accidental insurance cover active.
disbursementSchedule: Overdraft limit activated after 6 months of active account operations; insurance disbursed on claim.

name: PM-Cares for Children Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
description: Provides comprehensive care and support for children who lost both parents, surviving parent, legal guardian, or adoptive parents due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
benefits: Fixed deposit of Rs. 10 Lakh created in the child's name in post offices
Monthly stipend from age 18 to 23 years for education and personal needs
Lump sum fund of Rs. 10 Lakh handed over on attaining 23 years of age
Free health insurance under PM-JAY up to 18 years with premium paid by PM-CARES
eligibilityCriteria: Children who lost both parents or surviving parent during the period 11th March 2020 to 5th May 2022 due to COVID-19.
Must have been below 18 years of age on the date of death of parents.
amountDetails: Fixed corpus of Rs. 10 Lakh. Monthly stipend during age 18-23. Health cover of Rs. 5 Lakh/year. Education fees fully funded in government schools.
applicationSteps: District Magistrates identify eligible children with child welfare committees.
Register details on the PM Cares for Children portal (pmcaresforchildren.in).
Open joint post office account with District Magistrate as guardian.
Generate PM-CARES ID Card and Ayushman Bharat health card.
extraIntel: Childline Helpline: 1098.
The scheme covers private school admission fees and books under Right to Education (RTE) provisions.
applicationPeriod: Closed (For children orphaned during COVID window)
disbursementSchedule: Monthly stipends paid between ages 18-23; Rs. 10 Lakh maturity corpus paid on 23rd birthday.
benefitDuration: Until child reaches 23 years of age

name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
description: A maternity benefit program providing cash incentives to pregnant and lactating mothers for the first living child, compensating for wage loss and promoting health-seeking behavior.
benefits: Cash incentive of Rs. 5,000 for the first child paid in two tranches
Additional Rs. 6,000 paid for the second child (if girl child) to promote gender balance
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) directly into bank accounts linked with Aadhaar
Encourages institutional deliveries and complete child immunization
eligibilityCriteria: Pregnant Women and Lactating Mothers (PW&LM) for their first or second child.
Excludes government employees and those receiving similar benefits under other laws.
amountDetails: First child: Rs. 5,000 (Tranche 1: Rs. 3,000 on registration; Tranche 2: Rs. 2,000 after child birth and 1st immunization cycle). Second girl child: Rs. 6,000 in a single installment.
applicationSteps: Register at the local Anganwadi Center (AWC) or healthcare center.
Fill out Form 1A (for first child) or Form 1B on the PMMVY portal (pmmvy.wcd.gov.in).
Submit MCP Card details, antenatal check-up proofs, and Aadhaar card.
Biometrics and bank details are validated online for direct DBT transfer.
extraIntel: PMMVY Helpline: 011-23381611.
Register within 270 days of the Last Menstrual Period (LMP) date to claim the first tranche.
disbursementSchedule: Direct bank transfer within 30 days of submitting verification milestone proofs.

name: PM Programme for Restoration, Awareness, Nourishment and Amelioration of Mother Earth (PM-PRANAM)
ministry: Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers
description: An initiative to incentivize States and Union Territories to promote alternative fertilizers and balanced use of chemical fertilizers, helping restore soil health.
benefits: 50% of the subsidy savings passed to States as financial grants
Grants can be used for asset creation, technological adoption, and organic manure distribution
Promotes bio-fertilizers and organic alternatives to improve crop quality
Reduces financial burden of chemical fertilizer import subsidies
eligibilityCriteria: Implemented at the State/UT level to benefit agricultural communities.
Farmers adopting natural/organic farming or reduced chemical fertilizer usage.
amountDetails: Grants calculated based on 50% of the state's total chemical fertilizer subsidy savings in a fiscal year.
applicationSteps: Farmers participate through local panchayat and cooperative society fertilizer distribution cards.
Purchase organic or alternative bio-fertilizers from registered outlets.
State departments log chemical reduction yields to claim central subsidy grants.
extraIntel: The scheme does not have a separate budget; it draws from chemical subsidy savings.
disbursementSchedule: Annual central grants released to State treasury on verification of subsidy savings.

name: Pradhan Mantri Poshan Shakti Nirman (PM-POSHAN)
ministry: Ministry of Education
description: A centrally sponsored school meal program providing hot cooked meals to children in government and government-aided schools, improving nutritional status and attendance.
benefits: Free hot cooked nutritious mid-day meals every school day
Covers children in pre-primary (Balvatika) and Classes 1 to 8
Nutritional norms: 450 calories/12g protein for primary; 700 calories/20g protein for upper primary
Promotes school enrollment, retention, and social socialization among children
eligibilityCriteria: Children enrolled and attending government, government-aided, local body, or alternative education schools.
Aged between 5 and 14 years.
amountDetails: Fully sponsored hot school meals daily. Nutritional quality checked regularly.
applicationSteps: Enroll child in any government-run primary or upper primary school.
Child is automatically enrolled in the school mid-day meal program register.
Meals served daily during school recess hours.
extraIntel: Education Helpdesk: 1800-110-001.
Enabling kitchen gardens in schools is encouraged to supplement fresh organic micro-nutrients.
disbursementSchedule: Daily meal distribution at school centers.

name: PM Schools for Rising India (PM-SHRI)
ministry: Ministry of Education
description: A centrally sponsored scheme to showcase, develop, and upgrade over 14,500 schools across India into high-quality exemplar institutes embodying the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
benefits: State-of-the-art smart classrooms, labs, library, and sports infrastructure
Eco-friendly school structures (solar panels, water harvesting, waste management)
Modern experiential learning methodologies and student assessment toolkits
Career counseling and entrepreneurship training guidance for older students
eligibilityCriteria: Students enrolled in designated PM-SHRI exemplar schools selected by the Central and State governments.
Covers K-12 schooling levels.
amountDetails: Fully funded educational infrastructure upgrades. No direct cash to students, but fully subsidized modern schooling.
applicationSteps: Locate and select a designated PM-SHRI school in your district.
Apply for admission through standard school enrollment procedures.
Enrolled students automatically gain access to all upgraded infrastructure.
extraIntel: PM-SHRI Portal Support: wts-pmshri@gov.in.
Schools are selected based on challenge-method bidding. Track if your local school is selected on the portal.
disbursementSchedule: Infrastructure fund tranches disbursed directly to school management committees annually.

name: Stand-Up India Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Finance
description: Promotes entrepreneurship at the grassroots level by providing bank loans between Rs. 10 Lakh and Rs. 1 Crore to at least one SC/ST and one Woman borrower per bank branch.
benefits: Collateral-free bank loans ranging from Rs. 10 Lakh to Rs. 1 Crore
Covers greenfield enterprise projects in manufacturing, services, or trading sectors
Credit Guarantee Fund coverage to secure loans without private guarantors
Up to 7 years repayment tenure with maximum 18 months moratorium
eligibilityCriteria: SC/ST and/or Female entrepreneurs aged above 18 years.
Only for greenfield (new) enterprise setups in non-farm sectors.
Borrower must hold at least 51% shareholding in case of non-individual enterprises.
amountDetails: Loans between Rs. 10 Lakh to Rs. 1 Crore. Interest rate is lowest bank rate for category (MCLR + 3% + Tenor Premium).
applicationSteps: Visit Stand-Up Mitra portal (standupmitra.in) and register as an entrepreneur.
Fill out the eligibility test and select your required handholding support (if any).
Submit your project plan online to connect with public/private banks.
Bank processes credit checks and issues the loan sanction letter.
extraIntel: Stand-Up India Helpdesk: 1800-180-1111.
Ensure your project is a new (greenfield) unit. Existing business expansions are not eligible for this specific loan.
disbursementSchedule: Disbursed in tranches based on project machinery purchase and civil work milestones.

name: PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan
ministry: Ministry of Commerce and Industry
description: A digital platform to coordinate integrated planning and coordinated execution of infrastructure connectivity projects (railways, roadways, shipping) across India, reducing logistics costs.
benefits: Unified portal to plan logistics and infrastructure corridors
Reduces delays in project clearances and spatial data verification
Improves connectivity to industrial parks, manufacturing zones, and ports
Subsidized connectivity support benefits local businesses and exporters
eligibilityCriteria: Industrialists, infrastructure developers, logistics companies, and MSME clusters.
Registering projects seeking multimodal connectivity integration.
amountDetails: Logistics cost reduction benefits. Multimodal infrastructure grants coordinate over Rs. 100 Lakh Crore central master plan.
applicationSteps: Access the Gati Shakti portal and register your enterprise/MSME zone profile.
Submit requests or feedback regarding regional road/rail connectivity bottlenecks.
Logistics departments coordinate directly to resolve transport gaps.
extraIntel: DPIIT Gati Shakti Desk: logistics-dpiit@gov.in.
Utilize the portal's GIS spatial mapping tool to find the nearest cargo hubs and railway sidings for your business.
disbursementSchedule: Infrastructure project funds cleared in stages through Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs.

name: Prime Minister's Development Initiative for North Eastern Region (PM-DevINE)
ministry: Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region
description: A 100% centrally funded scheme for infrastructure development, social development projects, and livelihood opportunities for youth and women in the North Eastern States.
benefits: 100% Central funding for infrastructure projects in NE states
Livelihood support grants for local youth and women enterprises
Upgraded regional roads, tourist centers, and digital connectivity
Supports local traditional bamboo, handicraft, and handloom clusters
eligibilityCriteria: Resident citizens, local self-help groups, and youth startups located in the 8 North Eastern States.
Must propose projects aligned with regional development priorities.
amountDetails: Lump sum project grants funded 100% by central budget. Individual livelihood startup grants up to Rs. 5 Lakh.
applicationSteps: Submit project proposals through the state planning department or local DoNER portal.
Proposals are audited for regional connectivity and employment impact.
On approval, central grants are disbursed directly to project accounts.
extraIntel: DoNER Helpline: 011-23022400.
Focus is given to projects that generate massive employment for local women and school dropouts.
disbursementSchedule: Project funding released in installments matching civil construction milestones.

name: SVAMITVA Scheme (Survey of Villages and Mapping with Improvised Technology)
ministry: Ministry of Panchayati Raj
description: A collaborative scheme to map residential land property in rural areas using drone technology, providing 'Property Cards' (Title deeds) to rural household owners.
benefits: Issuance of official Property Cards (Gharouni) establishing absolute ownership
Enables rural citizens to use their property as collateral for securing bank loans
Reduces property disputes and land mutation delays in villages
Enables Gram Panchayats to prepare scientific development and taxation plans
eligibilityCriteria: Rural property owners possessing residential houses built in the inhabited (Abadi) areas of villages.
Residing in states where SVAMITVA survey has been notified.
amountDetails: Free drone survey and card generation. Property cards provided with zero survey cost to the land owner.
applicationSteps: Drone survey schedules are announced locally by district administration/Panchayat.
Reside on-site to mark property boundaries (Chuna marking) with local revenue officials.
Draft mapping sheets are published for objections/corrections.
Collect printed Property Card from local panchayat or download digitally.
extraIntel: Panchayati Raj Helpline: 1800-110-001.
Ensure all boundaries are clearly marked with lime (Chuna) during the drone flight to avoid mapping errors.
applicationPeriod: Rolling village schedules notified by State revenue departments
disbursementSchedule: Physical property cards handed over within 90 days of resolving mapping objections.

name: PM Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan (PM-JANMAN)
ministry: Ministry of Tribal Affairs
description: An umbrella mission focusing on 11 critical interventions to uplift Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) with housing, clean water, medical access, and skill training.
benefits: Assured permanent houses under PMAY-Gramin with increased Rs. 2.39 Lakh assistance
Connecting isolated tribal settlements with all-weather metalled roads
Free medical camps, mobile health units, and Ayushman Bharat card enrollments
Skill development centers and Van Dhan Vikas Kendras for forest produce processing
eligibilityCriteria: Families belonging to Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs) listed by the government.
Residing in identified isolated tribal habitations.
amountDetails: Free housing, clean water tap connections, solar lighting kits, and medical coverage up to Rs. 5 Lakh/year.
applicationSteps: District special officers conduct village-level survey verification camps.
Tribal beneficiaries are enrolled via offline biometric systems.
PMAY housing grants and solar lighting kits are dispatched directly to the settlement.
extraIntel: Tribal Affairs Support: 011-23381652.
Mobile network vans are dispatched regularly to tribal hamlets to issue on-the-spot caste certificates.
disbursementSchedule: Housing and infrastructure funds managed and disbursed directly by district officers to suppliers.

name: PM-eBus Sewa Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: An initiative to deploy 10,000 electric buses in 169 selected cities across India, upgrading public transit networks and promoting green, pollution-free urban mobility.
benefits: Deployment of modern, air-conditioned eco-friendly electric buses in tier-2 and tier-3 cities
Creation of dedicated charging infrastructure and bus depot substations
Provides safe, cheap, and clean public transport option for citizens
Generates green-jobs in bus operations, charging, and maintenance
eligibilityCriteria: Beneficiary public residing in targeted cities with a population of 3 Lakh to 40 Lakh.
Open to all citizens.
amountDetails: Public infrastructure scheme funded jointly by center and states. Affordable bus ticket fares set by local municipalities.
applicationSteps: Access e-bus services at municipal bus stops in selected cities.
Use local digital smart cards or mobile transit apps to purchase tickets.
extraIntel: MoHUA Public Transit Desk: support-ebus@gov.in.
Prioritizes cities lacking organized public bus transport services. Check if your city is listed on the MoHUA portal.
disbursementSchedule: Operational subsidies disbursed to bus operators monthly based on kilometers run.

name: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP)
ministry: Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers
description: An initiative to provide high-quality generic medicines at highly affordable prices to all citizens through dedicated retail outlets known as Janaushadhi Kendras.
benefits: High-quality generic medicines sold at 50% to 90% cheaper than branded equivalents
Sourced from WHO-GMP compliant manufacturers to guarantee therapeutic efficacy
Over 10,000 Janaushadhi Kendras active across India for easy access
Includes surgical items, health supplements, and sanitary napkins (Suvidha at Rs. 1)
documents: Doctor's prescription (required to buy prescription drugs)
eligibilityCriteria: Open to all Indian citizens looking for affordable healthcare medicines.
Prescription from a registered medical practitioner is required for regulated drugs.
amountDetails: 50%-90% savings on medicine bills. Generic medicines pricing managed strictly by NPPA.
applicationSteps: Locate your nearest Janaushadhi Kendra using the 'Janaushadhi Sugam' mobile app.
Present your doctor's prescription detailing generic names at the counter.
Purchase quality generic medicines at printed subsidized prices.
extraIntel: Janaushadhi National Toll-free: 1800-180-8080.
Ask your doctor to write generic names of medicines on prescriptions to make buying from Janaushadhi outlets easier.
disbursementSchedule: Subsidized sales settled instantly at counter checkout.

name: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
description: Organizes rural poor women into Self Help Groups (SHGs) and provides them with continuous financial support, skill training, and bank linkages to build diversified livelihoods.
benefits: Access to Revolving Fund (RF) of Rs. 20,000 and Community Investment Fund (CIF)
Interest subvention on bank loans reducing interest rates to 7% (or 4% on prompt repayment)
Extensive skill training for starting dairy, agriculture, sewing, or retail trades
Direct market access through regional SARAS fairs and online platforms
eligibilityCriteria: Rural women belonging to BPL or economically weaker households.
Must be a member of a Self Help Group (SHG) comprising 10-20 women.
amountDetails: Revolving Fund: Rs. 15,000 to Rs. 20,000 per SHG. Community Investment Fund: Rs. 1.10 Lakh per SHG. Direct low-interest loans up to Rs. 5 Lakh.
applicationSteps: Form or join a local Self Help Group (SHG) with 10-20 neighborhood women.
Open a joint savings bank account for the SHG and maintain regular records.
Apply for the Revolving Fund and bank credit linkages through your village coordinator.
Utilize funds collectively for micro-enterprise or household emergencies.
extraIntel: DAY-NRLM National Helpdesk: 1800-110-120.
Ensure the SHG holds regular weekly meetings and maintains clean ledger books to qualify for bank loan linkages.
disbursementSchedule: Funds credited directly to SHG savings bank accounts upon local federation verification.

name: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Urban Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NULM)
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: Aims to reduce poverty of urban poor households by enabling them to access gainful self-employment and skilled wage employment opportunities, and providing shelters for urban homeless.
benefits: Subsidized bank loans for self-employment (up to Rs. 2 Lakh for individuals, Rs. 10 Lakh for groups)
Free skill training in multiple trades under EST&P component
Interest subvention of 5% to 7% per annum on timely loan repayments
Upgraded street vending zones and support for urban homeless shelters
eligibilityCriteria: Urban poor, street vendors, ragpickers, and homeless citizens.
Annual family income must not exceed Rs. 1,00,000.
amountDetails: Individual loans: up to Rs. 2 Lakh. Group loans: up to Rs. 10 Lakh. Interest subvention reduces effective loan interest rates.
applicationSteps: Apply through the local Urban Local Body (ULB) office or online NULM portal.
Submit trade choice for free skill training or upload business plan for self-employment loan.
ULB task force screens applications and sponsors them to commercial banks.
On approval, bank releases loan tranches directly to vendor accounts.
extraIntel: NULM Helpdesk: support-nulm@gov.in.
Shelters for the homeless under NULM provide free clean bedding, water, and sanitation facilities year-round. Check location mapping on the site.
disbursementSchedule: Training fees paid to centers; self-employment loans disbursed within 15-30 days of bank approval.

name: Shyama Prasad Mukherji Rurban Mission (SPMRM)
ministry: Ministry of Rural Development
description: Focuses on developing clusters of villages that preserve the character of rural life while offering urban facilities like piped water, 24/7 power, digital connectivity, and hospitals.
benefits: Upgrades rural clusters with smart classrooms, sports arenas, and parks
Piped drinking water supply, paved roads, and modern sewage infrastructure
Local agro-processing, warehousing, and cold storage units to support farmers
Upgraded primary health centers and vocational skill centers inside villages
eligibilityCriteria: Residents of village clusters selected under the Rurban program.
Covers over 300 clusters nationwide.
amountDetails: Direct public infrastructure development. Critical Gap Funding (CGF) up to 30% of project cost funded by Center.
applicationSteps: Village panchayats submit cluster development projects to State government.
Rurban nodal agencies approve master layouts for transport, health, and solar utilities.
Upgraded facilities are managed collectively by the Rurban Cluster committee.
extraIntel: Rurban Helpdesk: rurban@gov.in.
Check the Rurban dashboard to see if your village cluster has been selected for smart infrastructure upgrades.
disbursementSchedule: CGF grants released in tranches matching infrastructure construction schedules.

name: Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT)
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: A national mission to ensure that every urban household has access to a tap with an assured supply of water, a sewerage connection, and upgraded green spaces/parks.
benefits: Piped household drinking water tap connections to urban households
Modern storm-water drainage systems to reduce urban street flooding
Upgraded municipal parks, walking tracks, and children's playgrounds
Promotes clean public transport and pedestrian-friendly pathways
eligibilityCriteria: Residents of over 500 notified cities and towns across India.
Open to all household property owners.
amountDetails: Free or highly subsidized household tap and sewerage connection installation.
applicationSteps: Submit a tap/sewerage connection application to your local municipal corporation.
Provide property address verification and identity details.
Municipal engineers verify on-site piping and install the connection.
extraIntel: AMRUT Support: support-amrut@gov.in.
Check with your local ward office if your colony is covered under the current sewer network upgrade phase.
disbursementSchedule: Municipal plumbing works scheduled matching neighborhood project cycles.
nextMilestoneDate: AMRUT 2.0 city water balance audits: November 2026

name: Smart Cities Mission
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: An urban renewal program to develop 100 cities across India, making them citizen-friendly, technologically advanced, and sustainable through smart solutions.
benefits: 24/7 smart water and electricity grid tracking systems
Integrated Command and Control Centers (ICCC) for city safety and traffic management
Upgraded smart roads, public Wi-Fi zones, and green utility parks
Improved municipal e-governance services for quick certificate issues
eligibilityCriteria: Residents and businesses operating in the 100 designated Smart Cities.
Open to all urban citizens.
amountDetails: Infrastructure master plan coordinate over Rs. 2,00,000 Crore investment.
applicationSteps: Access municipal services digitally via your smart city's official e-governance app.
Register civic complaints or request utility services directly on the portal.
extraIntel: Smart Cities Mission Desk: smartcities@gov.in.
Use the local Smart City Portal to track live bus schedules, book parking spots, or apply for trade licenses.
disbursementSchedule: Infrastructure grants released to Special Purpose Vehicles (SPV) matching development milestones.
nextMilestoneDate: Evaluation of smart corridor completions: June 2026

name: Swachh Bharat Mission - Urban (SBM-U)
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: Aims to make all cities Open Defecation Free (ODF), achieve 100% scientific solid waste management, and construct individual household and community toilets.
benefits: Financial subsidy of Rs. 4,000 to Rs. 12,000 to construct Individual Household Latrines (IHHL)
Access to clean Community Toilets (CT) and Public Toilets (PT) in commercial zones
Efficient door-to-door waste collection and segregated garbage transport services
Swachhata App access for quick waste dump cleaning reports
eligibilityCriteria: Urban households lacking a toilet facility inside their residence.
Must have space to construct a toilet or apply for community toilet access.
amountDetails: Subsidy grant up to Rs. 4,000 (Central share) plus state matching (typically totaling Rs. 8,000 to Rs. 12,000) paid directly in two tranches.
applicationSteps: Register on the SBM-Urban portal (sbmurban.org) under IHHL application.
Upload photo of applicant and front page of bank passbook.
Local inspectors verify on-site space and approve the first construction tranche.
Upload photo of completed toilet structure to claim the second tranche.
extraIntel: SBM-U Toll-free: 1969.
Use the Swachhata mobile app to take photos of garbage piles in your neighborhood; municipal teams are mandated to resolve them within 24 hours.
disbursementSchedule: Tranche 1 (50% value) paid at verification; Tranche 2 (balance) paid on completion.

name: Swachh Bharat Mission - Gramin (SBM-G)
ministry: Ministry of Jal Shakti
description: Focuses on rural sanitation, providing financial incentives to rural households to construct individual toilets, and managing solid and liquid waste in villages.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 12,000 to construct an Individual Household Latrine (IHHL)
Covers costs of twin-pit construction ensuring waste decomposes safely on-site
Promotes clean village surroundings with drainage and compost pits
Supports women safety by eliminating the need for open defecation
eligibilityCriteria: Rural households lacking a domestic toilet.
Includes BPL families, and APL families belonging to SC/ST, small/marginal farmers, landless laborers, and physically challenged.
amountDetails: Financial grant incentive of Rs. 12,000 paid directly into the beneficiary's bank account.
applicationSteps: Submit application to local Gram Panchayat secretary or apply online on SBM-G portal.
Panchayat reviews eligibility and conducts physical space check.
Construct toilet using prescribed twin-pit design guidelines.
Biometric upload of geotagged photo of completed toilet triggers subsidy release.
extraIntel: SBM-Gramin Helpdesk: ddws-sbm@gov.in.
Twin-pit design is recommended: it converts waste into rich, dry manure over 12 months, which can be safely used in farming.
disbursementSchedule: One-time direct bank transfer (DBT) post-construction geotagged photo verification.

name: Jal Jeevan Mission (Har Ghar Jal)
ministry: Ministry of Jal Shakti
description: A national initiative to provide safe and adequate drinking water through individual Functional Household Tap Connections (FHTC) to all households in rural India by 2024 (extended).
benefits: Assured clean drinking water tap connection inside your rural home
Guarantees minimum 55 liters of safe water per person per day
Regular testing of water quality managed by local village water committees
Relieves rural women from the chore of walking long distances for water
eligibilityCriteria: Rural households situated in village habitations across India.
Open to all village resident homes.
amountDetails: Free tap installation. Subsidized water supply maintenance managed locally by Paani Samitis.
applicationSteps: Panchayat Paani Samiti registers household requirements.
Water department lays public distribution pipes in village streets.
Tap connection is fitted inside the house and water quality is tested.
extraIntel: Jal Jeevan Mission Toll-free: 1800-11-0707.
Locals are trained to test water quality using field testing kits (FTKs). Ask your local Anganwadi worker to run checks.
disbursementSchedule: Subsidized tap installation executed on pipeline completion.

name: PM-KUSUM (Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan)
ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
description: Allows farmers to install solar water pumps, grid-connected solar power plants, and solarize existing diesel pumps, ensuring clean energy and additional farm income.
benefits: 60% government subsidy (30% center, 30% state) on solar water pumps
Farmers only pay 10% of pump cost; remaining 30% available as bank loan
Provides reliable daytime irrigation power, replacing expensive diesel fuel
Farmers can sell excess solar power back to the grid for extra income
eligibilityCriteria: Individual farmers, group of farmers, water user associations, and cooperatives.
Must own cultivable land with a stable water source (well/borewell).
amountDetails: 60% direct subsidy grant on solar pump cost. Up to 30% loan option. Excess power sold to DISCOM at notified tariff.
applicationSteps: Register on your State's renewable energy agency portal (linked via mnre.gov.in).
Submit land maps, borewell depth details, and select solar pump capacity.
Pay your 10% farmer share online upon allocation.
Nodal agency installs the solar pump and registers DISCOM power feed.
extraIntel: PM-KUSUM Support: 1800-180-3333.
Beware of fake websites. Only apply through official state government renewable portals listed on the MNRE main page.
applicationPeriod: Annual allocations released state by state
disbursementSchedule: Pump installation and setup completed within 45-60 days of paying the farmer share.

name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)
ministry: Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying
description: A flagship scheme to bring about the 'Blue Revolution' through sustainable development of the fisheries sector, upgrading infrastructure, and supporting fishers.
benefits: Financial assistance up to 40% (general) or 60% (SC/ST/Women) to start aquaculture units
Subsidies for buying deep-sea fishing vessels, motorboats, and bio-floc ponds
Insurance cover for fishers and boats against accidents and natural disasters
Support for cold storage, ice plants, and fish retail markets
eligibilityCriteria: Fishers, fish farmers, fish workers, self-help groups, and cooperative societies.
Must possess active fish farming license or own suitable water body land.
amountDetails: 40% (General) or 60% (Women/SC/ST) subsidy grant on capital costs. Accident insurance up to Rs. 5 Lakh.
applicationSteps: Submit project proposal to the local District Fisheries Office (DFO).
Enter design specs (e.g. pond size, bio-floc setup, boat capacity).
DFO conducts field inspections and sanctions the subsidy package.
Execute project and submit receipts to claim DBT subsidy transfer.
extraIntel: Fisheries Helpdesk: 011-23380313.
The scheme promotes seaweed cultivation as an alternative livelihood for coastal women. Training is fully sponsored.
disbursementSchedule: Subsidy released in tranches directly to bank accounts post-milestone audits.

name: Soil Health Card Scheme
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: Provides printed cards to farmers containing details of soil nutrients (macro, micro) and recommendations on appropriate fertilizer dosages for their land to optimize yields.
benefits: Free soil testing conducted by government labs every 2 years
Detailed report on 12 key soil parameters (Nitrogen, Phosphorus, pH, etc.)
Reduces excess fertilizer spending, improving soil health and crop yields
Customized crop-wise fertilizer dosage recommendations
eligibilityCriteria: All landholding farmers across India who own cultivable land.
Open to all crop growers.
amountDetails: 100% free soil sample collection, laboratory analysis, and printed card dispatch.
applicationSteps: Agriculture field officers collect soil samples from your farm plot.
Samples are analyzed in district soil testing laboratories.
Soil Health Card is printed and delivered directly to the farmer, or accessed on portal.
extraIntel: Soil Health Portal Support: support-soil@gov.in.
Avoid sampling soil immediately after fertilizer application to get an accurate baseline report.
applicationPeriod: Twice a year (Pre-Kharif and Pre-Rabi seasons)
disbursementSchedule: Soil health cards printed and dispatched within 30 days of sample collection.
benefitDuration: Card valid for 2 Years (New testing recommended after)

name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY)
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: Promotes organic farming through a cluster-based approach, providing financial support to farmers for organic input production, certification, and marketing.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 50,000 per hectare over 3 years
Free organic certification under Participatory Guarantee System (PGS)
Subsidy for organic inputs (bio-fertilizers, vermicompost, botanical extracts)
Support for packaging, branding, and local marketing of organic produce
eligibilityCriteria: Farmers willing to form clusters of 20 hectares (minimum 20-50 farmers) and adopt organic methods.
Must transition land from chemical to organic farming.
amountDetails: Rs. 50,000 per hectare grant over 3 years, with Rs. 31,000 (62%) paid directly to farmers for organic inputs.
applicationSteps: Form an organic farming cluster of 20-50 farmers in your village.
Register the cluster on the local organic portal under Participatory Guarantee System (PGS-India).
Attend local organic input preparation training.
Claim fertilizer input subsidies annually directly in your bank account.
extraIntel: Organic Farming Helpdesk: organic-dac@gov.in.
Transitioning to organic takes 3 years to get full certification. Be patient and preserve crop records.
applicationPeriod: Annual cluster registration window (April to June)
disbursementSchedule: Direct bank transfer (DBT) released in annual installments over the 3-year certification cycle.

name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY)
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: Focuses on 'Per Drop More Crop' by promoting water-saving micro-irrigation systems (drip and sprinkler systems), optimizing farm water usage.
benefits: Up to 55% (small/marginal farmers) or 45% (other farmers) subsidy on drip/sprinkler systems
Reduces water usage by up to 50% and improves fertilizer uptake efficiency
Subsidized construction of farm ponds, check dams, and water harvesting structures
Increases crop yield quality through precise moisture delivery
eligibilityCriteria: All landholder farmers possessing agricultural land with an active water source.
Must choose approved micro-irrigation systems from empaneled manufacturers.
amountDetails: Subsidy grant covers 45%-55% of total system invoice value. Remaining paid by farmer or bank loan.
applicationSteps: Apply online on your state's micro-irrigation portal (e.g. MADA, HADA).
Upload land maps and borewell proof.
Nodal manufacturer designs system layout and submits invoice estimate.
Pay your farmer share; manufacturer installs drip/sprinkler systems on-site.
extraIntel: PMKSY Support: 1800-180-1551.
Ensure regular filter cleaning in drip lines to prevent clogging from sandy borewell water.
disbursementSchedule: Subsidy released to empaneled manufacturer directly post-installation verification check.

name: National Beekeeping & Honey Mission (NBHM)
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: Supports the development of scientific beekeeping (Sweet Revolution) to increase crop pollination, improve honey production, and generate income for rural youth.
benefits: Subsidies up to 40% to buy bee colonies, hives, and honey extractors
Free scientific training on bee management, colony division, and disease control
Grants to establish honey testing labs, processing plants, and packaging units
Enhances crop yields of local farms by improving natural cross-pollination
eligibilityCriteria: Farmers, rural youth, women self-help groups, and cooperative societies.
Willing to undergo scientific beekeeping training.
amountDetails: Subsidy up to 40% on hive sets (maximum Rs. 80,000 subsidy per beneficiary). 100% free training.
applicationSteps: Apply through the State Horticulture Department or National Bee Board portal.
Attend the mandatory 7-day basic beekeeping training course.
Purchase hive sets and bee colonies from registered breeders.
Submit purchase bills to horticulture officer to claim subsidy DBT.
extraIntel: National Bee Board Desk: 011-23382432.
Place hives near mustard, sunflower, or eucalyptus farms during bloom cycles to double both honey yields and crop pollination.
applicationPeriod: Annual training batch enrollments (June to August)
disbursementSchedule: Subsidy released to bank accounts within 30 days of invoice verification.

name: Pradhan Mantri Dakshata Aur Kushalata Sampanna Hitgrahi (PM-DAKSH)
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: A national skill development scheme providing free skill training, stipends, and placement support to SC, OBC, Safai Karamcharis, and denotified tribes.
benefits: 100% free skill development training in multiple modern sectors
Monthly stipend of Rs. 1,000 (OBC/EBC) or Rs. 1,500 (SC/Safai Karamcharis) during training
Self-employment loan guidance and direct placement assistance
Government-recognized Skill Certificate on completion
eligibilityCriteria: Indian citizens belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC), Other Backward Classes (OBC) with income < Rs. 3 Lakh, Economically Backward Classes (EBC) with income < Rs. 1 Lakh, or Safai Karamcharis.
Aged between 18 and 45 years.
amountDetails: Free training. Monthly stipend up to Rs. 1,500/month based on course type and category.
applicationSteps: Register on the PM-DAKSH portal or mobile app.
Select your category and choose from available courses (up-skilling, short-term, entrepreneurship).
Upload caste, income, and Aadhaar documents.
Attend the nearest government-designated training institute on selection.
extraIntel: PM-DAKSH Helpdesk: support-daksh@gov.in.
The scheme has a mandatory 80% biometric attendance requirement to qualify for the monthly stipend payout. Be punctual.
applicationPeriod: Annual registration windows (typically May to July)
disbursementSchedule: Stipend credited directly to Aadhaar-seeded bank accounts monthly.

name: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
description: Provides credit guarantees to financial institutions to facilitate collateral-free and third-party guarantee-free business loans to micro and small enterprises.
benefits: Enables collateral-free business loans up to Rs. 5 Crore
Covers both manufacturing and service sector businesses
Trust covers up to 85% of default default loan risk, reducing bank lending hesitancy
Special higher guarantee cover and lower fees for women-owned enterprises
eligibilityCriteria: New and existing Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) seeking bank credit.
Excludes retail trade (except specified), educational institutions, and agricultural units.
amountDetails: Credit guarantee cover up to Rs. 5 Crore. Guarantee fee ranges from 0.37% to 1.35% per annum.
applicationSteps: Obtain MSME Udyam Registration online (udyamregistration.gov.in).
Prepare a detailed business project report and approach any commercial bank.
Apply for loan and request coverage under the CGTMSE trust scheme.
Bank evaluates project and applies directly to CGTMSE for guarantee coverage.
extraIntel: CGTMSE Helpdesk: 022-61437800.
Udyam registration is 100% free. Never pay agents. A clean business plan with realistic financial projections is key to bank approval.
disbursementSchedule: Guarantee coverage approved within 7-10 days of bank project recommendation.
benefitDuration: Valid for the tenure of the business loan

name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
description: A credit-linked subsidy program to generate employment opportunities in rural and urban areas by setting up new micro-enterprises (manufacturing and services).
benefits: Financial subsidy up to 35% (rural) or 25% (urban) on project cost
Covers projects up to Rs. 50 Lakh (manufacturing) and Rs. 20 Lakh (service)
Only 5% (special category) or 10% (general) beneficiary contribution required
Subsidized loan processed through commercial banks with easy interest terms
documents: Education certificate (8th pass required for high value)
eligibilityCriteria: Any individual entrepreneur aged 18 years or older.
Must have passed at least 8th standard class to apply for projects > Rs. 10 Lakh (mfg) or > Rs. 5 Lakh (service).
Only for new (greenfield) micro-enterprises.
amountDetails: Subsidy rate: 15%-35% based on location and category. Margin money (subsidy) kept in a 3-year lock-in FD.
applicationSteps: Apply online on the KVIC PMEGP portal.
Select sponsoring agency (KVIC, KVIB, or DIC) and upload project DPR.
Sponsoring agency forwards approved projects to commercial banks.
Bank sanctions loan, releases funds, and claims margin money subsidy.
extraIntel: PMEGP National Helpdesk: 1800-3000-0034.
A mandatory 10-day Entrepreneurship Development Programme (EDP) training must be completed online/offline before loan release.
disbursementSchedule: Margin money subsidy released to bank branch on loan sanction; locked for 3 years before adjustment.
nextMilestoneDate: District level task force committee meetings monthly

name: ASPIRE Scheme (A Scheme for Promotion of Innovation, Rural Industries and Entrepreneurship)
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
description: Sets up Livelihood Business Incubators (LBIs) and Technology Business Incubators (TBIs) to train rural youth, foster innovations, and create local enterprises in agro-rural sectors.
benefits: Free incubation and hands-on skill training in agro-business and manufacturing
Financial assistance up to Rs. 1 Crore to setup Livelihood Business Incubators
Grants and seed capital up to Rs. 8 Lakh for eligible startup entrepreneurs
Supports development of innovative technologies in agro-processing
eligibilityCriteria: Rural youth, MSME units, private partners, and educational institutions.
Startups working in agro-rural industries sector.
amountDetails: 100% free incubator training. Seed capital grants up to Rs. 8,000,000 for innovative business plans.
applicationSteps: Apply as a candidate at any designated ASPIRE Livelihood Business Incubator (LBI).
Submit enterprise startup plan to the LBI project manager.
Undergo incubation training and apply for seed capital grants on project clearance.
extraIntel: ASPIRE Nodal Desk: aspire-msme@gov.in.
LBIs offer practical training on operating agro-machinery (like mini-flour mills, packaging systems). Utilize this to build real-world skills.
applicationPeriod: Rolling batch schedules at local LBIs
disbursementSchedule: Incubator grants released in tranches matching setup machinery procurement.

name: SFURTI Scheme (Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries)
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
description: Organizes traditional artisans and craftspeople into clusters to make them competitive, providing raw material support, common facility centers, and market branding.
benefits: Setup of Common Facility Centers (CFCs) with modern machinery for artisans
Financial assistance up to Rs. 2.5 Crore (Regular clusters) or Rs. 5 Crore (Major clusters)
Free design inputs, skill upgrades, exposure visits, and packaging training
Market linkages through exhibitions, trade fairs, and online e-commerce setups
eligibilityCriteria: Traditional artisans, weavers, potters, coir workers, and honey gatherers.
Must form or join a registered cluster group comprising at least 500 artisans.
amountDetails: 100% grant for CFC machinery and capacity building. Individual artisan training is fully sponsored.
applicationSteps: Artisans join their regional registered SFURTI cluster group.
Nodal agencies (like KVIC, Coir Board) prepare a Detailed Project Report (DPR).
Common Facility Centers are constructed and equipped with modern machinery.
Artisans use the CFC collectively to process and package their products.
extraIntel: SFURTI Helpdesk: sfurti@gov.in.
Check the portal directory to find an active cluster in your district for coir, khadi, bamboo, or pottery products.
disbursementSchedule: CFC project funds released in 3 tranches matching civil construction milestones.

name: PM-SURAJ (Pradhan Mantri Social Utthan and Rozgar Hamrahi)
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: A national portal to provide direct credit support (subsidized loans) and welfare benefits to marginalized sections including Scheduled Castes, OBCs, and sanitation workers.
benefits: Direct channel to apply for subsidized business loans up to Rs. 15 Lakh
Lower interest rates starting at 4% to 6% per annum
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) of loan tranches to bank accounts without intermediaries
Ayushman health cards and safety gear kits provided for sanitation workers (Safai Karamcharis)
eligibilityCriteria: Marginalized individuals belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), Other Backward Classes (OBC), or Sanitation Workers (Safai Karamcharis).
Annual family income must not exceed Rs. 3,00,000.
amountDetails: Loans up to Rs. 15,000,000. Interest rates subsidized between 4.00% to 6.00% based on project size.
applicationSteps: Visit the PM-SURAJ portal and click 'Apply for Credit Support'.
Enter Aadhaar details and select caste/occupational category.
Submit business choice (e.g. e-rickshaw, retail shop, small tailoring unit).
Verify documents online; funds are disbursed directly by state channel partners.
extraIntel: PM-SURAJ Support: support-suraj@gov.in.
Sanitation workers applying under this scheme can also get free safety training under the NAMASTE scheme.
disbursementSchedule: Disbursed directly to state financial development corporations for onward DBT transfer within 15-20 days.

name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maan-Dhan Yojana (PM-KMY)
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
description: A government pension scheme providing social security to small and marginal farmers by offering a monthly pension of Rs. 3,000 on reaching 60 years of age.
benefits: Assured monthly pension of Rs. 3,000 upon reaching 60 years of age
50% voluntary contribution by the farmer; matching 50% contributed by Central Government
Family pension of 50% (Rs. 1,500/month) to spouse in case of subscriber death
Auto-debit facility from PM-Kisan installment accounts available
eligibilityCriteria: Small and marginal farmers owning cultivable land up to 2 hectares.
Aged between 18 and 40 years.
Must not be covered under other social security schemes (like NPS, EPFO, ESIC).
amountDetails: Monthly pension of Rs. 3,000. Monthly subscriber contribution ranges from Rs. 55 to Rs. 200 based on entry age.
applicationSteps: Visit nearest Common Services Centre (CSC) or apply online at maandhan.in.
Provide Aadhaar card, bank details, and land registry copy.
Calculate monthly contribution matching your age and complete auto-debit consent.
Receive Kisan Pension Card with unique subscriber number.
extraIntel: Maandhan Helpdesk: 1800-3000-3400.
Farmers can choose to pay their monthly contributions directly out of their PM-Kisan payout installments.
disbursementSchedule: Pension disbursed on the 1st of every month to beneficiary bank accounts post age 60.

name: Atal Bhujal Yojana (ATAL JAL)
ministry: Ministry of Jal Shakti
description: A community-led groundwater management scheme implemented in water-stressed blocks of 7 Indian states, promoting drip irrigation and water budget planning.
benefits: Subsidized water harvesting, recharge pits, and check dam construction
Free community water budget planning and groundwater measuring kits
Increases regional agricultural borewell sustainability and crop output
Encourages community-led water saving via village Water Security Plans
eligibilityCriteria: Farmers and rural households situated in notified water-stressed groundwater blocks within the 7 participating states.
Willing to participate in water security training.
amountDetails: Fully sponsored community infrastructure and training. Individual water harvesting sub-grants up to Rs. 25,000.
applicationSteps: Register details with the local village Gram Panchayat water security committee.
Participate in regional water budget planning meetings.
Incorporate micro-irrigation guidelines to claim state agriculture incentive bonuses.
extraIntel: ATAL JAL Helpdesk: support-ataljal@gov.in.
Priority is given to village plans that allocate separate water budgets for cattle drinking pools and crop irrigation.
disbursementSchedule: Infrastructure funding disbursed in tranches to local water user associations.
nextMilestoneDate: Village water security plans review: March 2027

name: National Pension Scheme for Traders, Shopkeepers and Self-Employed Persons
ministry: Ministry of Labour and Employment
description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme providing social security to retail traders, shopkeepers, and self-employed persons in India.
benefits: Assured monthly pension of Rs. 3,000 upon reaching 60 years of age
50% subscriber contribution; matching 50% central government contribution
Spouse family pension of 50% in case of subscriber death post-retirement
Simple, self-declaration based enrollment with no complex audit paperwork
eligibilityCriteria: Self-employed shopkeepers, retail traders, rice mill owners, commission agents, etc.
Annual turnover must not exceed Rs. 1.5 Crore.
Aged between 18 and 40 years.
amountDetails: Monthly pension of Rs. 3,000. Monthly premium contribution ranges from Rs. 55 to Rs. 200 based on age.
applicationSteps: Visit any nearest Common Services Centre (CSC) with Aadhaar and bank passbook.
